channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Man United crashes out of the FA Cup but Amorim says winning the Premier League is his ultimate goal
item.description: Manchester United crashed out of the FA Cup on Sunday after losing on penalties to Fulham. But rather than bemoan the latest blow in a miserable season, United coach Ruben Amorim insisted his sights were set on a bigger trophy. “The goal is to win the Premier League.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jaden Agassi, son of tennis legends Andre Agassi and Steffi Graf, works to make mark in baseball
item.description: TUCSON, Ariz. (AP) — It's not the least bit surprising that Jaden Agassi — son of tennis legends Andre Agassi and Steffi Graf — had a racket in his hand from the time he could walk. The little boy loved to run. He loved to hit the ball. The problem was accuracy wasn't his thing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pitt football player, HSE grad dies in Fishers crash
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS — An 18-year-old football player died in a crash in Fishers late Saturday night. According to a press release sent by the Hamilton County Sheriff's Office Sunday morning, the crash happened around 9:03 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Hudson, NewsNation

item.title: Kansas' Zeke Mayo shows screenshots of racist, hateful messages he received after Texas Tech loss
item.description: LAWRENCE, Kan. (AP) — Kansas guard Zeke Mayo showed screenshots of racist, hateful messages he received following his performance in a loss to No. 10 Texas Tech. Mayo shared the screenshots Saturday. His account on X appeared to be deactivated Sunday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After 5 years in jail, ex-gang member Peake wins New Zealand Open and qualifies for British Open
item.description: QUEENSTOWN, New Zealand (AP) — Ryan Peake, an Australian former motorcycle gang member who turned his life around through golf, earned a place at the British Open on Sunday when he won the 104th New Zealand Open by one stroke.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Homophobic chant at San Diego FC's inaugural home match condemned by coach, sporting director
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — San Diego FC coach Mikey Varas and sporting director Tyler Heaps expressed disappointment and anger after their club's inaugural home match was marred in the second half by three occurrences of the homophobic chant frequently heard at the Mexican national team's soccer matches.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: San Diego FC makes its home debut before a sellout crowd in a city hungry for top-level soccer
item.description: A match decades in the making finally happens Saturday night when San Diego FC debuts on its home field in front of a sellout crowd of fans starved for elite professional sports — and particularly hungry for top-level soccer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: No. 7 St. John's clinches first Big East regular-season title in 40 years and eyes more celebrations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The final horn sounded and St. John’s skipped cutting down the nets. The players instead held the trophy after the No.
item.dc:creator: LARRY FLEISHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: School district bans trans athletes from girls' sports as state defies Trump, sparking loud pro-trans chants
item.description: The Tumwater School District in Washington voted Thursday to ban trans girls from playing for girls’ sports teams despite the state continuing to allow it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lone Peak wins fourth straight state championship
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The dynasty continues for the Lone Peak girls basketball team. The Knights won their fourth straight state championship Thursday beating Copper Hills for a second straight year, 46-42.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: NFLPA Survey: Matt Eberflus rated worst head coach in 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The NFL Players Association released the results of their annual players survey Wednesday at the NFL Scouting Combine.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
